operating_state: Total production reached 2,389 MBOED in Q1 2025, increasing 26% year-over-year driven by Marathon Oil acquisition completed November 2024 and organic growth from new wells across multiple regions. Average realized price declined to $53.34 per BOE from $56.60 per BOE due to lower crude oil prices, partially offset by higher natural gas prices from cold winter weather demand.
cost_structure: Operating cash flow generation of $6.1 billion demonstrates maintained cost discipline despite production increases. Capital expenditures totaled $3.4 billion allocated primarily to flexible short-cycle unconventional plays. Full-year capital expenditure guidance lowered to $12.3-$12.6 billion from $12.9 billion while maintaining production guidance, indicating improved capital efficiency from Marathon Oil integration.
material_events: Completed Marathon Oil acquisition integration with expected synergies exceeding $1 billion run rate within first full year. Closed noncore asset dispositions generating $1.3 billion proceeds. Returned $2.5 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends. Declared second-quarter ordinary dividend of $0.78 per share. Became sole operator of Kebabangan Cluster PSC in Malaysia and achieved first oil at Gumusut Phase 4.
macro_exposures: Crude oil prices declined in Q2 2025 due to broad-ranging U.S. tariffs and retaliatory measures with significant uncertainty regarding scope and duration. OPEC Plus actions and geopolitical tensions create ongoing supply disruption risks affecting global demand and inventory levels.
forward_outlook: Second-quarter 2025 production guidance set at 2.34-2.38 MMBOED with full-year production maintained despite lowered capital expenditure guidance. Management targets $2 billion in disposition proceeds with $1.3 billion secured. Willow project achieved critical winter construction milestones. Marathon Oil integration remains on track to deliver synergies exceeding $1 billion run rate within first full year.
uncertainty_profile: Commodity price volatility continues with external factors including global economic health, supply disruptions, military conflicts, environmental regulations, and weather-related disruptions beyond company control. Regional natural gas marker volatility expected throughout 2025 with ongoing third-party pipeline constraints affecting realized prices. Ultimate impact of tariff policies and geopolitical tensions remains uncertain.
